name: Basic Whip
description: The foundational 8-count pattern. Follower comes toward leader, redirects, and returns down the slot.
mechanics: ["1-2: Follower walks toward leader (walk walk)","3&4: Leader redirects follower with body movement (triple step)","5-6: Follower walks back down the slot (walk walk)","7&8: Both anchor (triple step) — stretch at end of slot"]
commonMistakes: ["Leader using arm instead of body to redirect","Follower not committing to walking toward leader","Missing the redirection timing on 3","Not getting to the end of the slot for the anchor"]

checklistTemplates.item: Maintain consistent frame tension throughout the pattern
Match partner's energy — don't overpower or go limp
Smooth hand transitions — no grabbing or dropping
Elbows stay in front of body, never behind
Shoulders down and relaxed, not hunched
Wrists neutral — not broken or overly rigid
Weight forward over balls of feet, not on heels
Core engaged — stable center of gravity
Head up, eyes forward — not looking at feet
Triple steps are evenly spaced (not galloping)
Walk steps hit clearly on the beat
Anchor completes before next pattern begins
Rhythm matches the music, not just counting
Body moves with the music — not robotic
Free hand has intentional placement
Smooth transitions between patterns — no jerky movements

name: Change of Places
description: Leader and follower swap ends of the slot. Both travel through each other's position.
mechanics: ["1: Leader steps back, begins to open slot","2: Both begin passing each other","3&4: Leader and follower pass each other in the slot","5&6: Both anchor at opposite ends"]
commonMistakes: ["Collision in the middle of the slot","Not committing to the full slot change","Losing connection during the pass","Anchor at wrong end of slot"]

name: Left Side Pass
description: Follower passes leader on the left side through the slot. Foundation for many variations.
mechanics: ["1: Leader steps back left, initiates lead for follower to pass","2: Leader steps right, clearing the slot","3&4: Leader triple steps to end of slot, follower walks through","5&6: Both anchor — follower now at opposite end of slot"]
commonMistakes: ["Leader not clearing the slot early enough","Follower not walking in a straight line through the slot","Lost connection during the pass","Not maintaining frame while passing"]

name: Push Break
description: Leader pushes follower out and brings them back. Builds understanding of compression and leverage.
mechanics: ["1-2: Both walk together with compression","3&4: Push out — leader extends, follower stretches away","5&6: Both anchor — follower at full stretch"]
commonMistakes: ["Using arms instead of body for the push","Not maintaining connection during the extension","Follower not matching the energy of the push","Anchor without real stretch"]

name: Starter Step
description: The standard way to begin social dancing. Two walks and a triple step to establish connection.
mechanics: ["1-2: Walk walk, establishing rhythm and connection","3&4: Triple step together","5-6: Walk walk","7&8: Triple step, settle into slot"]

name: Sugar Push
description: The foundational 6-count pattern. Leader pushes follower away, follower walks forward and anchors.
mechanics: ["1: Leader steps back left, follower steps forward right","2: Leader steps back right, follower steps forward left","3&4: Leader triple steps in place, follower walks forward and begins anchor","5&6: Both anchor in place — triple step with stretch and compression"]
commonMistakes: ["Follower not walking through the slot","Leader not creating clear compression on 1-2","Rushing the anchor — not letting the stretch develop","Leader backing up too far instead of just weighting back"]

name: Sugar Tuck
description: A tuck turn variation of the sugar push. Follower turns under the arm after the push.
mechanics: ["1: Leader steps back, creating compression","2: Leader redirects follower for the turn","3&4: Follower executes inside turn, leader triple steps","5&6: Both anchor — reestablish connection"]
commonMistakes: ["Leader forcing the turn with the arm","Follower anticipating the turn before the lead","Lost connection after the tuck","Not completing the turn before the anchor"]

name: Tuck Turn

name: Sailor Shuffle
description: Cross-behind step pattern that creates a bouncy, sailing motion. Great for upbeat music.
mechanics: ["1: Cross right behind left (or vice versa)","2: Step side","3&4: Sailor shuffle triple","5&6: Anchor with the sailor feel"]
commonMistakes: ["Cross too big — losing balance","Not maintaining the bouncy quality","Losing the slot","Not matching the music's bounce"]

checklistTemplates.item: Maintain consistent frame tension throughout the pattern
Match partner's energy — don't overpower or go limp
Smooth hand transitions — no grabbing or dropping
Elbows stay in front of body, never behind
Shoulders down and relaxed, not hunched
Wrists neutral — not broken or overly rigid
Weight forward over balls of feet, not on heels
Core engaged — stable center of gravity
Head up, eyes forward — not looking at feet
Triple steps are evenly spaced (not galloping)
Walk steps hit clearly on the beat
Anchor completes before next pattern begins
Rhythm matches the music, not just counting
Body moves with the music — not robotic
Free hand has intentional placement
Smooth transitions between patterns — no jerky movements

name: Sliding Door

mechanics: ["1-2: Both begin moving toward each other","3&4: Both slide past each other — slight body rotation","5-6: Continue to opposite ends","7&8: Both anchor at new positions"]
commonMistakes: ["Not enough space — collision","Timing mismatch on the pass","One person sliding, other walking","Not maintaining the slot"]

name: Cuddle Wrap
description: Similar to sweetheart but both arms wrap. Creates a cozy side-by-side position.
mechanics: ["1: Begin turning follower into cuddle","2: Both arms cross into cuddle position","3&4: Walk together in cuddle hold","5&6: Unwind and anchor"]
commonMistakes: ["Arms twisted uncomfortably","Not traveling together smoothly","Unwind direction confusion","Too much tension in the hold"]

name: Self Wrap
description: Leader wraps themselves instead of the follower. Unexpected and fun.
mechanics: ["1: Leader initiates self-wrap","2: Leader crosses arm across own body","3&4: Leader turns into the wrap","5&6: Unwrap and anchor"]
commonMistakes: ["Getting tangled in own wrap","Losing connection with follower","Follower confused about what to do","Not unwinding smoothly"]

name: Shadow Wrap
description: Follower ends up behind leader in shadow position through a wrap movement.
mechanics: ["1-2: Leader draws follower around","3&4: Follower moves behind leader into shadow position","5-6: Both move together in shadow hold","7&8: Follower exits shadow, anchor"]
commonMistakes: ["Follower can't see where they're going","Leader forgetting follower is behind them","Entry into shadow too rough","Exit not clean"]

name: Waist Wrap
description: Follower wraps around leader's waist or vice versa. Creates close body connection.

name: Wrap Turn
description: A wrap that transitions into a turn for the unwinding. Combines two techniques.
mechanics: ["1-2: Leader initiates wrap","3&4: Follower turns into the wrap","5-6: Continue turning to unwrap","7&8: Exit cleanly, anchor"]
commonMistakes: ["Turn direction wrong — wrapping tighter instead of unwrapping","Not enough turn for a clean exit","Lost count during the wrap-turn","Connection break"]
